chunk_summary: Structured, family-based dietary and activity interventions for pediatric obesity focus on reducing sugar, refined grains, and screen time, while promoting balanced meals, physical activity, and scheduled follow-ups.
learning_objectives: Understand stepwise dietary modifications for managing pediatric obesity, including portion control and macronutrient balance.
Learn the importance of physical activity, reduced screen time, and sleep hygiene in obesity management.
Identify appropriate follow-up schedules and escalation criteria for pediatric obesity management.
Recognize when to refer to multidisciplinary programs or consider advanced interventions like pharmacotherapy or surgery.
key_facts: Eliminate sugar-sweetened beverages, energy drinks, and routine fruit juice from the diet.
Use the 'plate rule': half vegetables/fruit, one-quarter staple (roti/rice), and one-quarter protein (dal, egg, fish, etc.).
Encourage at least 60 minutes/day of moderate to vigorous physical activity.
Follow-up should occur every 4–6 weeks initially, then every 1–3 months based on risk.
Escalate intervention if no BMI improvement is seen after 3–6 months of adherence.
Severe obesity may require referral for pharmacotherapy or metabolic surgery in specialized centers.
reference_quote: Effective obesity management is structured, family-based, and behavior-focused. (IAP Revised Guidelines on Evaluation, Prevention and Management of Childhood Obesity, 2023)
document_key_topics: Physical activity and screen time reduction
Follow-up and escalation in obesity management
